name: RENAL ABSCESS
definition: PYOGENIC RENAL ABSCESS = KIDNEY ABSCESS usually complication of renal inflammation with liquefactive necrosis; 2% of all renal masses
dominant_finding: well-defined hypoattenuating irregular focal round renal mass:
discriminating_features: well-defined hypoattenuating irregular focal round renal mass:
thick rim / halo of enhancing wall / pseudocapsule
no enhancement of center of abscess

CECT: well-defined hypoattenuating irregular focal round renal mass:
thick rim / halo of enhancing wall / pseudocapsule
no enhancement of center of abscess
US: slightly hypoechoic (early), hypo- to anechoic (late) mass
MR: heterogeneous thick-rimmed complex T1-hypointense and T2-hyperintense lesion replacing
surrounding thick hypointense rim on T2WI
loss of psoas margin / obscuration of renal contour
respiratory immobility of kidney = renal fixation
